## Why this is (mostly) a no-op

v1's exit gate: do NOT re-fire on the same duplicate-document signal if v1's analysis still applies, and do NOT re-fire on a readiness-report `.md` write alone (ACT-V3-01 still pending).

This fire matches the first clause: the duplicate-document signal is **still live** because consolidation has not landed. v1 §4 edit proposals remain canonical. Re-running §1–§5 would produce identical proposals.

**What v5 adds beyond v2/v3/v4:** one new empirical finding (regression — see below) plus the now-confirmed pathological-loop status that v4 predicted.

## NEW FINDING — regression in `planning_artifacts/` (underscore)

v1 §4 explicitly directed: **delete `eusolicit-docs/planning_artifacts/` after merging unique content.** Since v4 was written (18:57), the underscore-directory files have been **modified, not deleted**:

| File                                          | Mtime               | Delta from v4   | Direction      |
| --------------------------------------------- | ------------------- | --------------- | -------------- |
| `planning_artifacts/PRD.md`                   | 2026-04-24 18:59:21 | **+2 minutes**  | ❌ Edited      |
| `planning_artifacts/ux-spec.md`               | 2026-04-24 19:04:24 | **+7 minutes**  | ❌ Edited      |
| `planning_artifacts/architecture.md`          | 2026-04-24 15:50:49 | (pre-v4)        | unchanged      |

**Interpretation:** Some agent or human edited the underscore-directory files **two and seven minutes after v4 was written telling everyone to delete that directory**. This is anti-progress — every edit to the underscore copy widens the divergence gap with the canonical hyphen copy that v1 §4 mandated as source of truth.

**Likely root cause:** an upstream skill (PM, tech writer, or document-edit workflow) is configured to write to the underscore path. Until that path is removed from whatever skill/template still references it, deletion will be reverted on next skill run.

**Action to add to the v1 carry-forward list:**

- **ACT-V5-01 (P0)** — Before deleting `planning_artifacts/`, grep the codebase for any tool, skill, template, hook, or agent prompt that writes to the underscore path. Fix the writer first; otherwise the directory will resurrect.
  - Search: `rg -l 'planning_artifacts[^-]' .` in project root (escape the hyphen char-class to find non-hyphen variants)
  - Likely culprits: BMAD skill templates, any old `_bmad/custom/*.toml`, orchestrator configs, or stale CLAUDE.md references.

---

## Trigger analysis (what fired this run?)

No new readiness report has been written since v4 (`implementation-readiness-report-2026-04-24-v4.md`, 16:04). So **this fire is NOT a readiness-report-write trigger** — that breaks the v2/v3/v4 pattern.

Most plausible triggers, ranked:

1. **Underscore-dir edits at 18:59 / 19:04** — file-watch trigger picking up content changes in `eusolicit-docs/`. Plausible if drift detection watches the whole docs tree.
2. **Orchestrator timer / schedule** — drift check on a fixed cadence regardless of signal. Plausible given the ~3.5h gap from v4 (18:57) to v5 (~22:15) is suspiciously close to a 4h cadence.
3. **Sprint-change-proposal write itself (v4)** — circular trigger if `correct-course`'s own output is in the watched set. Possible but should already have been excluded.

**Recommendation:** instrument the orchestrator to log the actual file/event that fires `correct-course`. Without that, root-cause analysis on each fire is guesswork.

---

## Pathological loop — confirmed

v4's exit prediction:

> If a v5 no-op fire occurs today without any mitigation, the Orchestrator team should treat it as a confirmed pathological loop and consider halting drift-detection until ACT-V3-01 lands.

**v5 has now fired. The condition v4 set is met.**

| Metric                   | After v2 | After v3 | After v4 | **After v5** |
| ------------------------ | -------- | -------- | -------- | ------------ |
| Total fires today        | 2        | 3        | 4        | **5**        |
| Substantive fires        | 1 (v1)   | 1 (v1)   | 1 (v1)   | **1 (v1)**   |
| Redundancy rate          | 50%      | 66%      | 75%      | **80%**      |
| Mitigation landed        | No       | No       | No       | **No**       |

The redundancy rate is asymptoting toward 100% — every additional fire adds noise without changing the canonical action set.

---

## Action — None for the project; **emergency action for the orchestrator team**

**For project artifacts (PRD/Epics/Architecture/UX/Stories):** No new edit proposals. v1 §4 remains the canonical change set. ACT-V5-01 added above to address the underscore-dir resurrection root cause.

**For the orchestrator team — recommended emergency mitigations, in priority order:**

1. **HALT drift-detection for the rest of 2026-04-24** until ACT-V3-01 lands. Five fires on the same signal in one day is a clear runaway condition. Continued firing wastes budget and pollutes the audit trail.
2. **Within the next orchestrator deploy:** implement debounce — suppress `correct-course` if the previous fire (a) was a no-op AND (b) occurred within the last 4 hours. Cheap one-liner; prevents v6.
3. **Next sprint:** land ACT-V3-01 (exclude readiness-report writes from drift triggers) AND add the trigger-event logging recommended above. ACT-V3-02 (wire drift threshold) remains the structural fix.
4. **Add to ACT-V3-01 scope:** also exclude sprint-change-proposal writes from drift triggers (otherwise this very file may trigger v6).
